page: Microsoft Azure FX12-6ms_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the FXmsv2 family, designed for memory-intensive workloads. It features 6 vCPUs mapped to 3 physical Intel Xeon 8573C cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). The server is equipped with 252.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 42.0 GB per physical core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, though it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark results show strong single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2350.0 and single-threaded gzip compression at 34.92 GB/sec, both placing in the top tier. Multi-core performance is moderate, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 6664.64 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 7274.0, reflecting the 6 vCPU limitation. Memory bandwidth is measured at 517.99 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 50.68 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. In database operations, the server achieves 9.49 million operations per second on Redis SET benchmarks and a Passmark database score of 4361.08. Large-scale LLM inference is a notable bottleneck, with the 7B model text generation dropping to 6.65 tokens per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the FX12-6ms_v2 offers high memory density relative to its compute footprint, making it suitable for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing. The lack of local storage requires external storage attachments, which may impact overall deployment costs. This configuration is suited for in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis, text processing applications, and lightweight single-threaded compression tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure FX12-6ms_v2 is a memory-optimized x86_64 server from the FXmsv2 family, powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor. It features 6 vCPUs across 3 physical cores, 252.0 GB of RAM, and a 260 MB L3 cache, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The server exhibits top-tier single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2350.0 and single-threaded gzip compression of 34.92 GB/sec. However, its multi-core capabilities are moderate due to the low core count, as shown by a stress-ng multi-core score of 6664.64. With an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 42.0 GB per core, this server is designed for memory-intensive workloads. It is best suited for in-memory databases, caching platforms like Redis, and text processing applications that benefit from high memory capacity and strong single-threaded execution.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX12-6ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 6 vCPUs, 252 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, delivering strong single-core performance for in-memory databases.

tagline: A memory-intensive server combining a high-frequency Intel Xeon processor with 252 GB of RAM for memory-bound applications.

252.0 GB of RAM providing a high 42.0 GB per core memory density
Top-tier single-core performance with a Geekbench score of 2350.0
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Suited for in-memory databases, Redis caching, and text processing
